title: Sales Commission Calculator - Commission & Net Pay
shortDescription: Calculate sales commission and net amount from a sale value and rate.
description: A free sales commission calculator that figures your commission and the net amount after commission from any sale value and rate. Perfect for salespeople, real estate agents, and freelancers.
metaDescription: Use this free sales commission calculator to find your commission and net amount instantly. Enter the sale amount and rate to see exact figures for any deal.

result.empty: Enter a sale amount and commission rate to see your results.

sections.heading: How commission is calculated
paragraphs: Commission is the slice of a sale that goes to the seller as payment. The formula is simple: commission = sale amount × rate ÷ 100. The net amount is what remains: sale amount − commission.
For example, a $10,000 sale at a 5% rate earns $500 in commission, leaving $9,500 net.

sections.heading: Who uses a commission calculator
paragraphs: Salespeople, real estate agents, and freelancers all rely on commission math to understand their earnings and plan their finances.
Knowing your commission ahead of time helps you set goals, compare offers, and confirm payments are correct.

howTo.name: Enter the sale amount
howTo.text: Type the total value of the sale or deal into the sale amount field.

howTo.name: Enter the commission rate
howTo.text: Type the commission rate as a percentage, such as 5 for 5%.

howTo.name: Read your results
howTo.text: The calculator instantly shows your commission and the net amount after commission.

faq.a: Multiply the sale amount by the rate, then divide by 100. For a $10,000 sale at 5%, the commission is $500.

faq.q: What is the net amount after commission?
faq.a: The net is the sale amount minus the commission. On a $10,000 sale at 5%, the net is $9,500.

faq.q: Can I use this for real estate commissions?
faq.a: Yes. Enter the property sale price and the agent's commission rate to see the commission and net proceeds.

faq.q: Does it work for any currency?
faq.a: Yes. The calculator works with raw numbers, so it applies to any currency you use.
Yes, it is completely free to use with no sign-up required.
